title: DERELICT
byline: Mining vessel ‘Kestrel-9’. You don't remember going under.

cryobay.title: Cryo-bay C
cryobay.text:
Cold. The kind that has teeth. You come apart from sleep one nerve at a time, lid of the cryopod hissing back over your face. The bay is dark except for a single amber fault-light, ticking on, off, on.

Eleven other pods line the wall. Ten of them are open. Empty. The readout above yours blinks a date you don't trust.

choices.text: Stay still. Listen to the ship first.

listen.text:
You hold your breath and let the vessel speak. The hull pops as it cools. Somewhere far down the spine of the ship, something drags — a long, wet, unhurried sound — and stops, as if it heard you holding still.

You decide not to listen anymore.

cryobay_up.title: Cryo-bay C
cryobay_up.text: Your legs remember walking before you do. On the wall, an emergency locker hangs half-open, its seal cracked. Beyond the bay, a corridor runs fore and aft into the dark.

choices.text: Force the emergency locker the rest of the way open.

locker_done.title: Cryo-bay C
locker_done.text: Inside: a hand torch, still charged, and a single med-gel ampoule — the field kind, for when a mind starts to slip its moorings. You take both. The light feels like a small, stupid kind of courage.

corridor.title: Spinal corridor
corridor.text: The corridor is a throat of dead consoles and floating dust. Three ways lead off it: the bridge fore, the med-bay to port, engineering aft. And straight ahead, a stretch where the lights are out entirely — a black gap the torch beam won't quite fill.

sanityText.40: The corridor breathes. You know corridors don't, you know it, but the walls swell and settle and the dust hangs like it's deciding something. Three doorways. A black gap ahead that is looking back.

choices.text: Push into the dark stretch ahead.

bridge.title: Bridge
bridge.text: The viewport is full of stars that aren't any you know. The captain's chair is turned to face the door, empty. The log terminal still has power; the last entry is timestamped, then nothing.

bridge_log.title: Bridge
bridge_log.text: The captain's voice is calm in the way of someone who has already given up. “We woke it in the ore. It doesn't want the ship. It wants us awake when it— ” The recording ends in a sound you will hear again behind your eyes for as long as you have eyes.

medbay.title: Med-bay
medbay.text: The med-bay smells of antiseptic and something underneath it. Two crew lie on the slabs under sheets that were white once. A supply cabinet hangs ajar.

choices.text: Lift the sheet on the nearest slab.

medbay_cab.title: Med-bay
medbay_cab.text: One med-gel ampoule rolls to the back of the cabinet. You pocket it. Small mercy.

choices.text: Lift the sheet on the nearest slab.

medbay_sheet.title: Med-bay
medbay_sheet.text: You shouldn't have. The body is intact and entirely wrong — the face fixed mid-expression, not pain, not fear. Attention. Whatever happened, this one was paying attention right to the end. You let the sheet fall.

engineering.title: Engineering
engineering.text: The reactor is a cold black slab. Main power is offline — that's why the ship is a tomb. A sealed access panel could bring it back, but it's bolted shut. A toolbox lies spilled across the deck plating.

sanityText.30: The reactor hums even though it's dead. You can feel it in your fillings. The panel is bolted shut. The toolbox has spilled, and the tools point — all of them — toward the dark stretch of corridor.

choices.text: Take the hydrospanner from the toolbox.

choices.locked: Open the reactor access panel. (bolted — need a tool)

engineering_power.title: Engineering
engineering_power.text: The panel comes free. You re-seat the breakers and the reactor catches with a deep, resentful hum. Down the corridor, lights stutter to life one bank at a time — racing toward the dark stretch, then dying again exactly where it begins.

darkgap.title: The dark stretch
darkgap.text: The gap swallows the torchlight whole. Air moves against your face — exhaled, not vented. Something is here with you, between you and the escape bay beyond.

choices.text: Raise the torch and look directly at it.
Kill the torch and feel along the wall in the black.

darkgap_lit.title: The dark stretch
darkgap_lit.text: You raise the beam. It's the captain — or it's wearing the captain — folded into the ceiling space, head tilted with that same terrible attention. It does not move toward you. It simply lets you see. A keycard hangs from the corpse's belt; you take it with hands that won't stop shaking, and it lets you.

darkgap_blind.title: The dark stretch
darkgap_blind.text: You kill the light and trust your hands. Halfway through, your fingers find a face on the wall at chest height — warm, smiling, patient — and it turns to follow your palm as you pass. You do not stop. You do not breathe. Something is missing from you when you reach the far side, and you don't know yet what it was.

escape.title: Escape bay
escape.text: One pod remains in its cradle, intact, waiting. Its console asks for two things: command authorisation, and power.

choices.text: Insert the captain's keycard and launch.

choices.locked: Insert authorisation. (no keycard — the captain had it)
Launch. (no power — the reactor is dead)

choices.text: Forget the pod. Open the outer airlock and walk out into the stars.

ending.text:
The pod tears free of the cradle. Through the port, the Kestrel-9 shrinks to a dead grey splinter against impossible stars — and on its hull, for just a moment, something turns to watch you go. Then distance takes it.

You are awake. You are alone. You are alive. Two of those will fade with time.

The stars are so kind. They've been calling the whole time and you only just heard. The outer door cycles, the air leaves in a single grateful breath, and you go to meet them smiling.

The ship logs the breach and powers down its last light.
